verified.name: Final Space
verified.tagline: Characters, episodes and locations from the Final Space cartoon
verified.description: A fan-built JSON API cataloguing the animated series Final Space: its characters, episodes, quotes and locations. Each character record carries stats, aliases, abilities and an avatar image URL. No key, open CORS, JSON out of the box.

verified.name: SWAPI
verified.tagline: Star Wars people, planets, ships and films as tidy JSON
verified.description: The classic Star Wars API: query people, planets, starships, vehicles, species and films through simple numbered endpoints. Each resource links to related records so you can crawl the whole galaxy. Keyless, CORS-open, plain JSON.
verified.notes: One of two SWAPI entries in this batch (swapi.dev host).

verified.name: SWAPI
verified.tagline: A maintained Star Wars API with wrapped, ID-based records
verified.description: An actively hosted Star Wars data API covering people, planets, films, starships and more. Responses wrap each entity in a result/properties envelope with a description and cross-links. No signup, open CORS, JSON only.

result.description: A person within the Star Wars universe

verified.notes: One of two SWAPI entries in this batch (swapi.tech host).

verified.name: US Weather
verified.tagline: Official US forecasts, alerts and observations from the NWS
verified.description: The US National Weather Service's public API: start from a latitude/longitude to resolve the forecast grid point, then pull forecasts, hourly data, active alerts and station observations. Data is US-government public domain. Keyless, though it wants a descriptive User-Agent.
verified.rateLimit: Unpublished (asks for a User-Agent identifying your app)

verified.name: wttr.in
verified.tagline: Weather as JSON (or ASCII art) for any place on Earth
verified.description: The console-friendly weather service, best known for its ASCII terminal output, also speaks JSON via the format=j1 query. One GET returns current conditions plus a multi-day forecast for a named location. No key needed and CORS is wide open.
verified.notes: Returns JSON with content-type text/plain; body parses as JSON.

verified.name: openSenseMap
verified.tagline: Live readings from citizen-run senseBox weather stations
verified.description: An open-data platform where hobbyist senseBox stations publish environmental measurements — temperature, humidity, particulate matter, UV and more. Fetch a single box to see its sensors and latest values, or query boxes by bounding box. Keyless and CORS-friendly.
verified.dataLicense: Open data (license set per senseBox; often public domain)
verified.notes: /boxes bounding-box queries can be large; single-box endpoint used for the probe.

verified.name: Cataas
verified.tagline: Cat as a service: random cat pics, gifs and metadata
verified.description: Cataas serves endless cat images and gifs, and with ?json=true it hands back a small JSON object describing the cat instead of the raw picture. Each record carries an id, tags, mimetype and a direct URL. No key, open CORS.

verified.name: RandomDuck
verified.tagline: A random duck picture URL on every request
verified.description: random-d.uk returns a JSON pointer to a random duck photo or gif each time you hit it. The payload is tiny: a friendly message and the image URL. No API key, though this endpoint did not advertise CORS headers.
verified.notes: No access-control-allow-origin header observed on this endpoint.

verified.name: RandomFox
verified.tagline: One random fox photo URL per call
verified.description: randomfox.ca answers each GET with a JSON object holding a random fox image URL and a matching web link. It's about as minimal as an API gets — handy for a quick placeholder image. Keyless with open CORS.

verified.name: MeowFacts
verified.tagline: Random cat facts as JSON, one or many at a time
verified.description: MeowFacts dishes out random feline trivia, returning a JSON object with a data array of fact strings. A count query parameter controls how many facts you get back. No key required and CORS is open.

data: Jaguars are the only big cats that don't roar.

verified.notes: Runs on a free Heroku-style dyno; responsive at check time but uptime isn't guaranteed.

verified.name: MCU Countdown
verified.tagline: How many days until the next Marvel release drops
verified.description: The engine behind whenisthenextmcufilm.com exposes a JSON endpoint counting down to the next Marvel Cinematic Universe title, with details on the upcoming and following productions. Alternate paths cover Star Wars, DC and the Batman universe. Data is drawn from TMDB; no key needed.
verified.notes: Movie data sourced from TMDB; TMDB attribution/terms apply. CORS header echoed the request Origin.

verified.name: ProblemsByVin
verified.tagline: Free NHTSA reliability datasets: failure mileage, recalls, more
verified.description: A set of static JSON datasets derived from tens of thousands of NHTSA owner complaints and recalls, covering failure-mileage quartiles, recall gaps, transmission and engine complaint density and more. Files are refreshed weekly and each ships with embedded methodology and licensing. Served as plain keyless JSON under CC BY 4.0.

verified.name: IMDbOT
verified.tagline: Unofficial keyless IMDb-style movie and series search
verified.description: A free, no-key search API that returns IMDb-style records for movies and shows — titles, years, IMDb IDs, cast and poster art — from a simple q query. It is the backend that powers the IMDbOT Telegram bot. Open CORS and JSON responses.
verified.notes: Unofficial; not affiliated with IMDb. Underlying IMDb data makes commercial reuse legally unclear. Live host found via the IMDbOT repo's homepage (FM-DB API).

skipped.name: CleanURI
skipped.reason: URL shortener is POST-only (GET returns HTTP 405); no keyless representative JSON GET endpoint.

skipped.name: GoTiny
skipped.reason: API host dead — gotiny.cc/api returns Vercel DEPLOYMENT_NOT_FOUND; creation is POST-only regardless.

skipped.name: Shrtcode
skipped.reason: Host dead — api.shrtco.de fails to connect (DNS/connection error, service shut down).

skipped.name: Dune
skipped.reason: Hosted API unreachable — heroku host returns 'No such app'; render.com host did not respond within timeout.

skipped.name: Movie Quote
skipped.reason: Hosted API dead — movie-quote-api.herokuapp.com returns 'No such app'; no live host documented.

skipped.name: Web Series Quotes Generator
skipped.reason: Serves quote images, not JSON, and its heroku host is dead ('No such app').

skipped.name: Pirate Weather
skipped.reason: Requires an API key — probe returned HTTP 401 Unauthorized without a registered key.

skipped.name: Axolotl
skipped.reason: Backend host dead — netlify site is docs-only (all endpoint paths 404) and the real backend axoltlapi.herokuapp.com returns 404.

skipped.name: Cat Facts
skipped.reason: Heroku host dead — cat-fact.herokuapp.com returns HTTP 503 Application Error.

skipped.name: RescueGroups
skipped.reason: Requires an API key per its developer guide; no keyless JSON GET endpoint.

skipped.name: xeno-canto
skipped.reason: API v2 removed (returns error); v3 requires an API key (HTTP 401 'Missing or invalid key').
